articles.title: Scientists Discover First Atmosphere Around a Rocky Planet That Could Support Life
articles.summary: For the very first time, astronomers have detected an atmosphere surrounding a rocky planet located inside a star's habitable zone — the region in space where conditions might allow liquid water to exist. This is considered a landmark moment in the search for planets beyond our solar system that could potentially support life. Scientists used powerful space telescopes to study the planet's surface and the gases surrounding it. While researchers have not found any signs of life yet, the discovery proves that small, rocky planets like Earth can hold onto an atmosphere even in distant star systems. Experts say this opens an exciting new chapter in our understanding of how common Earth-like worlds might be across the universe.

lv1: What did scientists discover around a rocky planet for the first time? (科學家首次在一顆岩石行星周圍發現了什麼？)
In what special region of space was this planet found? (這顆行星位於太空中哪個特別的區域？)
lv2: Why is finding an atmosphere around a rocky planet in the habitable zone considered so important for the study of life in space? (為什麼在宜居帶的岩石行星上發現大氣層，對研究太空生命如此重要？)
What tools did scientists use to make this discovery, and why are such tools necessary? (科學家用了什麼工具作出這個發現，為什麼需要這類工具？)
lv3: If scientists eventually confirm that a planet outside our solar system has both an atmosphere and liquid water, should humanity invest resources in trying to travel there or communicate with it? What are the benefits and risks? (如果科學家最終確認太陽系外有行星同時擁有大氣層和液態水，人類是否應投入資源嘗試前往或與之通訊？有何利弊？)

Some people argue that spending billions on space exploration is wasteful when Earth still has poverty and climate problems. Do you agree or disagree? Give reasons. (有人認為，當地球仍面對貧窮和氣候問題時，花費數十億於太空探索是浪費的。你同意嗎？請說明理由。)

articles.title: 香港書展昨日閉幕 七天共吸引近百萬人次入場
articles.summary: 一連七日的香港書展連同運動消閒博覽及零食世界於七月二十二日正式閉幕，合共吸引約九十九萬人次入場，比去年的入場人數有所上升。書展期間，每天均有大批家長帶同子女入場，選購各類圖書及文具。香港書展是本港每年一度的文化盛事，在香港會議展覽中心舉行，吸引來自世界各地的出版商及讀者參與。今年書展以閱讀推廣為主軸，鼓勵市民多閱讀，培養良好的閱讀習慣。

lv1: 今年香港書展一共吸引了多少人次入場？
香港書展在哪個場地舉行？
lv2: 為什麼香港書展每年都吸引大批家長帶子女入場？閱讀對學生有什麼好處？
今年書展的入場人數比去年多，你認為是什麼原因令書展愈來愈受歡迎？
lv3: 在電子書和網上閱讀非常普遍的今天，實體書展是否仍有存在的必要？請從不同角度分析。
書展以推廣閱讀為目標，但有人認為書展已變成購物活動多於文化活動。你認同嗎？社會應如何平衡兩者？

articles.title: Deadly Fire at Bangkok Bar Kills at Least 28 People, Dozens More Injured
articles.summary: A serious fire broke out late on Sunday night at a popular bar in the Chatuchak area of Bangkok, Thailand's capital city. The blaze caused at least 28 deaths and left more than 70 people injured. Emergency services rushed to the scene to rescue those trapped inside and to bring the fire under control. The venue was reportedly busy with customers at the time the fire started. Authorities have launched an investigation to determine the cause of the fire and to check whether the building met proper safety standards. Fires in crowded entertainment venues are a recurring concern across Southeast Asia, where building safety rules are not always strictly enforced.

lv1: In which city did the deadly bar fire take place? (這場致命的酒吧火災發生在哪個城市？)
How many people were reported to have died in the fire? (據報有多少人在火災中遇難？)
lv2: Why is it especially dangerous when a fire breaks out in a crowded venue like a bar or restaurant? (為什麼火災在酒吧或餐廳等人多的地方發生時特別危險？)
What kinds of safety rules should buildings open to the public follow to help prevent fires and protect people? (向公眾開放的建築物應遵守哪些安全規定，以預防火災和保護人員？)
lv3: After a major fire tragedy, governments often promise stricter enforcement of safety laws, but problems tend to repeat. What systemic changes would be most effective in preventing future disasters? (每當發生重大火災悲劇後，政府往往承諾加強執行安全法規，但問題卻重複出現。你認為哪些制度性改變最能有效防止未來的災難？)

Some argue that venue owners should face criminal charges when safety violations lead to deaths. Others say accidents are hard to prevent entirely. What is the right balance between personal responsibility and systemic accountability? (有人認為，若場所違反安全規定而導致人命傷亡，場所負責人應面對刑事指控；但也有人說意外難以完全避免。責任應如何合理分配？)

articles.title: 世界人工智能大會閉幕 中國AI發展引全球關注
articles.summary: 上周在上海舉行的世界人工智能大會（WAIC 2026）圓滿閉幕，吸引來自全球各地的科技企業、研究人員及政府代表參與。大會展示了中國在人工智能領域的最新研究成果及應用技術，涵蓋醫療、教育、交通及製造業等多個範疇。多家中國科技巨頭在大會上發布最新AI產品，顯示人工智能在日常生活中的應用愈趨廣泛。專家指出，人工智能的急速發展既帶來龐大的經濟機遇，同時亦引發社會對就業、私隱及安全等議題的關注與討論。

lv1: 世界人工智能大會今年在哪個城市舉行？
大會展示的人工智能技術應用於哪些生活範疇？
lv2: 為什麼各國政府和科技公司都非常重視人工智能的發展？它能為社會帶來什麼改變？
人工智能的發展為什麼會令部分人擔憂就業問題？這種擔憂是否合理？
lv3: 人工智能的快速發展對香港學生的未來有什麼影響？學生應如何準備自己，以應對AI時代的挑戰？
有人認為人工智能的發展應受到更嚴格的國際監管，以保障私隱和安全；也有人認為過度監管會阻礙科技創新。你認為應如何取得平衡？
